concerns.text: T010 and T011 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) and described as 'Stub/TDD-first' tests that depend only on T001. However, T011 explicitly tests 'temporal split integrity (no 2019 data in train)', which requires the existence of the split logic implemented in T015. A test verifying the *output* of T015 cannot be written or run before T015 is implemented. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule for the artifact (temporal split logic) and makes the [P] tag incorrect.

T019 and T020 are marked [P] and described as 'Contract test for model output JSON schema' and 'Integration test for time-series cross-validation split logic'. T020 tests the 'split logic' which is implemented in T022. T019 tests the 'model output schema' which is produced by T024 (Evaluation). These tests depend on the implementation of T022 and T024 respectively. They cannot be written or run in parallel with the implementation of those tasks as 'stub' tests because the schema and logic they verify do not exist yet. The [P] tag is invalid.

T030 and T031 are marked [P] and described as 'Contract test for statistical report schema' and 'Integration test for sensitivity analysis range sweep'. T030 verifies the schema of the statistical report generated by T037. T031 verifies the sensitivity analysis logic implemented in T034/T036. These tests depend on the artifacts produced by T034/T036/T037. They cannot be parallel-safe stub tests written before the implementation of the features they test.

T012b is listed as depending on T012, but the description 'Implement logic to document... if Synthetic Mode is triggered' implies it is part of the data loading logic. While the dependency is technically correct (T012 must run to trigger the mode), the task description suggests it might be better integrated into T012 or T017 (logging) to avoid a separate task for a simple conditional flag update, though this is a minor structural nit.
